description_long: This object is a weathered, industrial-style gas pump with a boxy, rectangular form and a prominent horizontal top section. The surface is predominantly dark gray metal, heavily rusted and worn, with patches of reddish-brown corrosion and faded paint. On the front, there are two rectangular display panels, one larger and one smaller, both appearing to be covered or painted over, and a recessed area that likely housed a payment or control interface. A hose with a nozzle is attached to the right side, hanging loosely, and there are no visible digital displays or branding. The sides and back are plain, showing more rust and wear, with no additional features or openings. The overall appearance suggests an older, possibly decommissioned gas pump, suitable for a gritty urban or post-apocalyptic setting.
description: A heavily rusted, boxy metal gas pump with faded panels and a hanging hose, showing significant wear and no visible branding.
description_view_0: Front view shows two faded display panels, a recessed control area, and a hose with nozzle hanging on the right side.
description_view_1: Right side view reveals the thickness of the pump and the hose hanging down, with extensive rust patches.
description_view_2: Rear view is plain, with no panels or hoses, just a flat, rusted metal surface.
description_view_3: Left side view is similar to the right but without a hose, showing the angular shape and more rust.
attribute_fuel_pumps: One visible, vintage-style fuel pump with a boxy metal body, two faded display panels, and a single hose with nozzle attached on the right side. No visible branding or digital displays.
